articles.description: The focus of the meeting is to ensure uninterrupted supply, stable logistics and efficient distribution across the country, and the government is taking proactive steps to this end, they said

articles.title: Middle East conflict: UK sees no evidence that Iran is targeting Europe with missiles, says cabinet minister
articles.description: Steve Reed, Britain's cabinet minister, on Sunday (local time) said that there is no evidence that Iran could target Europe. This comes a day after Israel Defense Forces (IDF) claimed that Tehran could now target cities like Paris, London, and Berlin, after it launched missiles at Diego Garcia

topStories.title: US-Israel, Iran conflict: HMS Anson, Britain's nuclear-powered submarine, arrives in the Arabian Sea, says report
topStories.description: HMS Anson, Britain's nuclear-powered submarine has arrived in the northern Arabian Sea as tensions in the Middle East soar. Equipped with Tomahawk missiles, the Royal Navy submarine left a port from Australia's Perth on 6 March, days after the US and Israel launched strikes against Iran

topStories.title: Iran claims it has shot down ‘invading’ US F-15 fighter jet near Hormuz Island: Watch video
topStories.description: Iran claims to have shot down an enemy F-15 fighter jet near Hormuz Island, with investigations ongoing. The US and other Middle Eastern nations have not commented on the situation, while Iranian media has released a video of the incident.

topStories.title: PM Modi chairs high-level energy meet, reviews power readiness as Trump warns attack over Hormuz Strait
topStories.description: Narendra Modi reviewed key energy sectors as crude prices reached $119 high, tensions in West Asia continued to persist.

topStories.title: 5,000,000,000 kg: The Amount Of CO2 Emitted In 14 Days Of US' War On Iran
topStories.description: About 20,000 buildings have been damaged by US-Israeli strikes in Iran, the Iranian Red Crescent reported. It released an estimated 2.4 million tonnes of CO2 equivalent (tCO2e).

topStories.title: What did the Supreme Court say about paid maternity leave?
topStories.description: Why did the Supreme Court strike down Section 60(4) of the Social Security Code, 2020? What did it say about maternity leave for adoptive mothers? What did the Court recommend that the Union government do regarding paternity leave?

topStories.title: ‘Colonialism did its job of isolating us’ – Colombia VP
topStories.description: Francia Marquez called for stronger Latin America–Africa ties to boost growth and challenge colonial legacies.

topStories.title: Latin American presidents criticise US actions
topStories.description: Latin American leaders met at the CELAC Summit in Colombia where President Petro called for a Middle East ceasefire.

topStories.title: US-operated Patriot missile likely involved in Bahrain blast on March 9 that injured over 30 people
topStories.description: A blast in Bahrain's Mahazza, injuring over 30 civilians, may have resulted from a U.S. Patriot missile intercepting an Iranian drone. Analysis suggests the missile detonated mid-air, causing extensive damage, while Bahrain's defenses failed to prevent the Iranian attack on a nearby oil refinery.

topStories.title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Velmurugan’s Tamilaga Vazhvurimai Katchi exits DMK alliance; rules out joining NDA

dailyNews.title: Trump weighing options for deploying thousands of ground troops in Iran

dailyNews.title: Intelligence chiefs' testimony is out of sync with Trump's talking points

dailyNews.title: Trump threatens to send ICE agents to airports amid TSA travel chaos
dailyNews.description: As major airports across the country continue to grapple with long lines due to TSA staffing shortages, President Trump is threatening to send ICE agents to airports where they’ll do security as soon as Monday. NBC News’ Valerie Castro reports.

dailyNews.title: Iran refuses to back down after Trump issues 48-hour deadline over Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: Tehran has threatened to escalate strikes on energy infrastructure and target critical water desalination facilities, should President Donald Trump make good on a promise to "obliterate" the country's power plants if it does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz

dailyNews.title: Museum sues Trump administration alleging it canceled grant on basis of race
dailyNews.description: An Underground Railroad museum in upstate New York alleged in a lawsuit Friday that the Trump administration unlawfully terminated its federal grant on the basis of race, pointing to President Donald Trump's efforts to dismantle diversity-focused initiatives.
